one_time_description: Settlement monies received during the year (non-recurring)
economic_notes: Flying Dust First Nation has business enterprises in Construction Services, Real Estate Services, Energy Services, and Retail Services accounted for by modified equity method. Market-based member loan program provides loans up to $30,000 at 4.99%. Ottawa Trust Fund holds capital and revenue trust monies. Railway remediation and drainage capital projects underway.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020. Eight business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method (FDB Fuels Inc., FDB Holding Corporation, FDB Gravel Inc., Flying Dust Power Corporation Ltd., Flying Energy Oil & Gas Limited Partnership, Flying Energy Inc., Snipe & Celly Pro Shop Inc., 625706 Saskatchewan Ltd.). Five entities fully consolidated including Flying Dust CMHC Housing Program, Flying Dust Market Garden Ltd., Flying Dust Housing Corporation, Flying Dust Community Development Fund. Net loss from business entities of $1,295,299 recorded. Settlement monies of $698,094 received (non-recurring). Loss on partnership obligation of $477,580 recorded. Contribution of land $55,000. Long-term debt note was truncated in source document. ISC specific claim term loan of $730,142 due on demand classified separately. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + MLTC CFS + MLTC Program Services + MLTC Health & Social + CMHC + Health Canada + Government of Canada + Government of Saskatchewan + Northwest School Division + MLTC RDI = 17598948+1219074+663924+2069000+435222+143750+122100+28381+386088+185000 = 22851487; osr adjusted accordingly.
